name: Building Production LLM Applications
description: A hands-on engineering track for integrating large language models into backend services. Covers API patterns, streaming, function calling, context management, caching, observability, cost control, and production readiness.
targetAudience: Backend and full-stack engineers who need to ship LLM-powered features at production scale

steps.title: LLM API Fundamentals
steps.description: Master the OpenAI / Anthropic / Gemini API contracts: completions, messages, roles, tokens, stop sequences, and error codes. Understand the request/response lifecycle.

substeps.title: Study: API reference for your target provider
Build: Minimal API client with retry and timeout
Verify: Handle all documented error codes gracefully

steps.title: Streaming Responses
steps.description: Implement server-sent events (SSE) streaming to display LLM tokens as they arrive. Handle partial JSON, stream cancellation, and connection drops.

substeps.title: Study: SSE protocol and LLM streaming APIs
Build: Backend streaming endpoint with proper headers
Build: Frontend consumer that renders tokens incrementally
Verify: Graceful handling of mid-stream cancellation

steps.title: Function Calling & Tool Use
steps.description: Give LLMs the ability to call your APIs, query databases, and use external tools. Implement multi-turn tool loops, validate inputs, and handle tool errors safely.

substeps.title: Study: Function calling schema and tool loop pattern
Build: Register 3 tools (search, lookup, action)
Build: Recursive tool loop with max-iterations guard
Build: Input validation before executing tool calls
Verify: Tool call injection attempt is rejected

steps.title: Context Window Management
steps.description: Handle long conversations and large documents within finite context windows. Implement summarization, sliding windows, context compression, and memory management strategies.

substeps.title: Study: Token counting and context window limits per model
Build: Conversation trimmer that preserves system + recent turns
Build: Summarize-and-compress for long contexts
Verify: Quality vs truncated-context baseline

steps.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
steps.description: Build a RAG pipeline end-to-end: document ingestion, chunking, embedding, vector retrieval, and context injection. Optimize for retrieval quality and latency.

substeps.title: Study: RAG architecture and retrieval quality factors
Build: Document ingestion and chunking pipeline
Build: Semantic retrieval with pgvector or Chroma
Build: RAG-augmented query endpoint
Verify: Hallucination rate drops with RAG context

steps.title: Prompt Caching & Latency Optimization
steps.description: Reduce latency and cost with prompt caching. Profile your LLM calls, identify bottlenecks, and apply caching, batching, and request coalescing.

substeps.title: Study: Anthropic/OpenAI prompt caching mechanics and pricing
Measure: Baseline latency and cost per request
Build: Semantic cache with TTL for repeated queries
Verify: Cache hit rate and p95 latency improvement

steps.title: Observability & Evaluation in Production
steps.description: Add the instrumentation needed to operate an LLM service: structured logging, latency tracing, cost tracking, quality metrics, and alerting on regressions.

substeps.title: Study: LLM observability tools (LangSmith, Langfuse, OpenTelemetry)
Build: Structured log for every LLM call (tokens, latency, cost)
Build: LLM-as-judge eval for production traffic sample
Build: Alert on quality regression or cost spike
Verify: Dashboard shows cost and quality trend over 7 days

steps.title: Security, Rate Limiting & Cost Control
steps.description: Harden your LLM service against abuse and runaway costs. Implement per-user rate limits, budget caps, output sanitization, and prompt injection defenses.

substeps.title: Study: OWASP LLM Top 10 attack vectors
Build: Per-user token budget with hard stop
Build: Input/output sanitization pipeline
Build: Rate limiter with graceful degradation
Verify: Injection attempt is caught and logged
